steps: Identify the y-intercept: all data points lie above $y = 0$ even at small $x$, so the line must have a positive y-intercept, eliminating choice D ($y = 100x$, y-intercept 0).
Estimate slope from the scatterplot: prices rise from roughly 200 at $x \approx 1$ to roughly 300 at $x \approx 2$, giving slope $\approx \frac{300 - 200}{2 - 1} = 100$.
Verify with choice B ($y = 100x + 100$): at $x = 1$, $y = 200$; at $x = 2$, $y = 300$; at $x = 2.5$, $y = 350$. All consistent with the plotted data.
choiceAnalysis:
• Choice A — '$y = 200x + 100$': Slope 200 predicts $y = 500$ at $x = 2$, far above the data cluster. ✗
  • Choice B — '$y = 100x + 100$': Slope 100 and intercept 100 predict values that align well with the plotted points across the full range. ✓
  • Choice C — '$y = 50x + 100$': Slope 50 predicts $y = 200$ at $x = 2$, below most of the data. ✗
  • Choice D — '$y = 100x$': y-intercept 0 means $y = 0$ when $x = 0$, but the data does not pass through the origin; all houses have a positive base price. ✗
That matches choice B — B is correct.
